onboarding.description: lunel brings your whole dev environment in your pocket
onboarding.completeDev: A complete dev environment in your pocket
onboarding.thingsWorth: A few things worth knowing before you dive in
onboarding.builtInPublic: Built in public. Every line on GitHub
onboarding.codeNeverTouches: Your code never touches our servers
onboarding.nextJsNode: Next.js, Node, React Native and more
onboarding.keepPrivate: Lunel keeps your session private by default, so you can connect and ship without extra setup.
onboarding.encryptedBetween: Encrypted between your device and machine.
onboarding.oneTimeQr: One-time QR pairing keeps each connection secure.
onboarding.noAnalytics: No analytics events or behavioral tracking.
onboarding.checkCode: Check the code and verify security.
onboarding.featureAiAgentsDesc: Run Codex and OpenCode straight from the app.
onboarding.featureBrowserDesc: A full browser with dev tools baked in
onboarding.featureCodeEditorDesc: A proper editor built for mobile
onboarding.featureFileExplorerDesc: Browse and manage your entire project tree
onboarding.featureTerminalDesc: Run commands, scripts, and builds from your device.
onboarding.featureGitDesc: Full Git workflow without leaving the app
onboarding.featureProcessManagerDesc: See and control every process on your machine
onboarding.featurePortManagerDesc: Know what's listening and shut it down fast
onboarding.featureApiTestingDesc: Test endpoints without leaving your phone
onboarding.featureResourceMonitorDesc: Live system stats with real-time graphs
onboarding.review1Text: Pushed a hotfix from my phone on the train and it just worked. Full terminal, real shell, everything. Can't believe this is free.
onboarding.review2Text: Every other app I tried felt like a toy. Lunel actually lets me work. The editor is solid, git is built in, and the QR connect is seamless.
onboarding.review3Text: Scanned the QR from my terminal and I was in my repo instantly. End to end encrypted too which gives me peace of mind. Highly recommend.
onboarding.leaveReviewPrompt: If Lunel looks good to you, leaving a quick review helps more developers find it

fontNormal.title: Normal Font
fontNormal.sampleText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
fontNormal.alertMessage:
Used for UI text, labels, and most readable content.

Choose a clean, balanced font for long reading sessions.
It has the biggest impact on overall app readability.

fontCode.title: Code Font
fontCode.alertMessage:
Used for code, terminal, and technical content.

Monospace helps align indentation and symbols.
Pick one that clearly distinguishes characters like 0/O and 1/l.

fontDisplay.title: Display Font
fontDisplay.alertMessage:
Used for headings and branding elements.

Best for titles and visual emphasis, not long paragraphs.
A stronger style here changes the app's personality quickly.

storage.title: Storage Explorer
storage.warningDesc: Modifying or deleting files here can cause data loss or break things. Proceed only if you know what you're doing.

bottomBar.mainRowDesc: Core plugins are locked. Tap the last slot to customize.
bottomBar.quickAccessRowDesc: Tap any slot to add or change plugins.
bottomBar.allPluginsInUse: All plugins are already in use

help.title: Help & Information
help.question1: How do I connect to a session?
help.answer1: Run `npx lunel-cli` on your machine, then scan the generated QR code from the app.
help.answer2: Open Home and scan a fresh QR code from a new CLI session to reconnect.
help.question3: Why is the scanner not working?
help.answer3: Enable camera permission in iOS Settings and make sure the QR code is fully visible.
help.answer4: Verify your machine is online, rerun `npx lunel-cli`, and scan the newest QR code.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Tell us what to improve
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what worked, what broke, or what should improve.

lunelConnect.cameraAccessDesc: This app uses the camera to scan a QR code to securely connect the app to your development environment or codebase.
lunelConnect.enterCodeDesc: Type the code shown in your terminal
lunelConnect.howToConnectSubtitle: Run one command, scan a QR, you're in
lunelConnect.step1Title: Open your terminal on your PC
lunelConnect.step1Desc: Navigate to the repository where you want Lunel to work
lunelConnect.step2Desc: First time in a repo it gives you a QR code to connect. Run it again and it just resumes the last session without a new QR. To reconnect, tap the previous session in the app
lunelConnect.step3Desc: A QR code and a short code appear in your terminal. Scan with your camera or type the code in the input field and you are in
lunelConnect.onceConnected: Once connected, your whole machine lives in your pocket. Ship from the couch, the toilet, anywhere
lunelConnect.errorGeneric: Something went wrong, please try again.

browser.consoleTitle: Console will stream native log rows here
browser.consoleDesc: This panel is reserved for level filters, search, expandable values, and high-volume log rendering that feels native inside the browser plugin.
browser.consoleBullet1: Levels for verbose, info, warning, and error
browser.consoleBullet2: Expandable structured values and stack traces
browser.consoleBullet3: FlashList-backed rows once real collection lands
browser.networkTitle: Network will show requests and drill-in detail
browser.networkDesc: The first real version will prioritize stable request capture, compact list rendering, and reliable detail sections for headers, bodies, and responses.
browser.networkBullet1: Method, status, type, size, and duration
browser.networkBullet2: Request detail for headers and payloads
browser.networkBullet3: Safe handling when metadata is partial
browser.elementsDesc: We are not copying desktop devtools blindly. This section will expose only what the app browser can support reliably and cleanly.
browser.elementsBullet1: Select only the primitives we can support well
browser.elementsBullet2: Fallback UI if live inspection is limited
browser.elementsBullet3: Breadcrumb and detail surfaces reserved in the layout
browser.resourcesDesc: This section is reserved for cookies, local storage, session storage, and other resource snapshots that the browser plugin can expose safely.
browser.resourcesBullet1: Sectioned cards instead of web-style tables
browser.resourcesBullet3: Stable empty and refresh states first
browser.infoDesc: This panel will show the metadata that helps orient the rest of devsole quickly without turning into a dumping ground.
browser.infoBullet3: Fast copy actions for debugging later
browser.proxiesDesc: Tracked localhost ports are shared across the whole app session, not per browser tab, so this section stays global while the rest of devsole remains page-specific.
browser.proxiesBullet1: Track localhost ports explicitly from here or via the CLI --extra-ports flag
browser.proxiesBullet2: See which tracked ports are currently open on the CLI machine
browser.proxiesBullet3: Only tracked ports are exposed on phone localhost
browser.proxyPortError: Enter a valid port between 1 and 65535.
browser.proxyHint: Tracked ports are shared across all tabs and open on phone localhost once the CLI reports them live.
browser.networkRequestsHint: Requests triggered from the active page will appear here.
browser.elementsLoadHint: Load the page or pick an element
browser.elementsPickerHint: Open the Elements tab on a loaded page or use the picker to inspect the current DOM tree.

terminal.micPermDesc: This app uses the microphone so you can speak commands instead of typing in the terminal. Voice is sent to our servers for transcription only — we don't store or log it. Please enable microphone access in Settings to use this feature.

editor.fileDeletedDesc: "{{title}}" was removed and is now read-only in the editor.
editor.onlyFilesAllowed: Only files can be opened in the editor
editor.binaryFilesNotAllowed: Binary files cannot be opened in the editor

explorer.sortFilterDesc: Adjust how files are sorted and shown
explorer.searchCodebaseTip: Type to search across the entire codebase
explorer.searchPressEnter: Press search on keyboard to run codebase search
explorer.noMatchesTip: Try a broader path or change the casing filter.
explorer.tapHitToOpen: Tap a hit to open the file
explorer.noMatchingPathsInWithFolders: No matching paths in {{path}} + child folders
explorer.tapSearchToFind: Tap search to find paths in {{path}}
explorer.tapSearchToFindWithFolders: Tap search to find paths in {{path}} + child folders

git.discardDesc: This will permanently discard all unstaged changes. This cannot be undone.

http.routeViaPCDesc: Allows localhost access and uses the connected CLI machine for outgoing requests.
http.clearHistoryDesc: Removes saved request and response history from this tool.
